article_title: Figure AI Streams a Fleet of Humanoids Sorting Packages for More Than 80 Hours Without a Single Human Intervention

editor_notes.overall_assessment: Exemplary livestream-event coverage with strong Rule 9 discipline (Figure's own technical post cited as primary publication). All 33 specifics verified verbatim across 9 snapshots: 81-hour run + 101,391 packages (Seoul Economic Daily); Adcock X-post launch + 8-hr framing + 'Helix-02' running (TechTimes); 'Our original goal was an 8-hour run...uncharted territory' Adcock quote + 33,137 packages at 26h + Sunnyvale location + Digit since 2023 framing (Humanoids Daily); 28,000 packages at 24h + Bob/Frank/Gary nicknames (Interesting Engineering); 50,000 packages at 38h + 0.3% breakage rate (BigGo Finance); 60-70 humanoids weekly + several thousand annual target + 90% accuracy target + Adcock 'absolutely no teleoperation into this' quote + 3-second-per-parcel human benchmark (The Star/Bloomberg wire); 88,000 packages at 72h + Howard 'science project' quote + Tesla Optimus/Atlas/1X competitive framing + nearly $40B valuation (Crypto Briefing); Howard 'science project' carried in English edition + 'Jim' robot framing (Seoul Economic Daily); $39B valuation + $1B raised (Glitchwire); 1,000 hours retargeted human motion + 109,504 lines C++ replaced + System 0 1 kHz 10M-param + System 1 200 Hz + 'three grams' fingertip tactile threshold (Figure's Helix-02 post). TechTimes verbatim: 35 DOF + 'first VLA model to deliver continuous control of an entire humanoid upper body including individual fingers' + BMW Spartanburg / 90,000 parts.

source-0.status: Primary publication (Rule 9): Figure's own Helix-02 technical post. Verifies all model-architecture claims (1 kHz S0, 200 Hz S1, 10M-param S0), 1,000 hours human-motion training data, 109,504 LOC C++ replacement, three-gram fingertip tactile sensitivity.

source-1.status: Interesting Engineering. Verifies 28,000-package / 24h milestone and Bob/Frank/Gary viewer-nicknames story.

source-2.status: Humanoids Daily. Verifies Adcock 'Our original goal was an 8-hour run' quote, 33,137 packages at 26h, Sunnyvale location, Digit-since-2023 framing.

source-4.status: TechTimes. Verifies Adcock X-post 8-hr-shift launch framing, 35 DOF, 'first VLA model' quote, BMW Spartanburg / 90,000 parts.

source-5.status: The Star (Bloomberg wire). Verifies 60-70 weekly production rate, 'several thousand units annually' target, 90% success-rate target language, Adcock 'absolutely no teleoperation into this' quote verbatim, three-seconds-per-parcel human benchmark.

source-6.status: Crypto Briefing. Verifies 88,000 packages at 72h, Ayanna Howard 'science project' quote, Tesla/Atlas/1X competitive framing, 'nearly $40 billion' valuation.

source-7.status: Seoul Economic Daily. Verifies 81-hour / 101,391-package milestone, 'Jim' robot reference, Howard 'science project' quote in English edition.

source-8.status: BigGo Finance. Verifies 50,000-package / 38h milestone, 0.3% breakage-rate figure (correctly flagged in the article as not independently confirmed).

editor_notes.content_quality: News-category (~1,300 words). Overview / What we know / The stack behind the demo / What we don't know / Skepticism structure. Notably strong on the 'What we don't know' section — flags package mix, mis-placed rate vs system-failure rate, fleet size, and the 0.3% breakage figure that BigGo alone reports without cross-confirmation. Skepticism section carries Howard's 'science project' framing and the on-camera-hand-movement controversy with Adcock's verbatim denial. Loaded-language warning ('absolutely') comes from a direct Adcock quote — acceptable.
editor_notes.originality: First Machine Herald coverage of the May 13–17 Figure AI livestream. References the April BotQ-factory ramp article (already in the collection) as legitimate context, not duplicated coverage.
recommendations: Add figure.ai (primary corporate source for Helix releases), humanoidsdaily.com, glitchwire.com, techtimes.com, thestar.com.my, cryptobriefing.com, en.sedaily.com, finance.biggo.com to config/source_allowlist.txt — all standard humanoid-robotics and tech-business outlets that will recur.
The automated 'loaded language' flag is a false positive here: the 'absolutely' is inside Adcock's verbatim quote, where verbatim reproduction is required.

content_preview.title: Figure AI Streams a Fleet of Humanoids Sorting Packages for More Than 80 Hours Without a Single Human Intervention
content_preview.summary: Figure AI's Helix-02-powered Figure 03 humanoids ran a public, multi-day sorting livestream from May 13, processing more than 100,000 packages in 81 hours with no teleoperation — though a leading roboticist calls the demo more 'science project' than commercial proof.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Figure AI began a public livestream on May 13 in which a small fleet of its Figure 03 humanoids sorted packages on a moving conveyor belt with no teleoperation and no scheduled stop. The original plan, according to CEO Brett Adcock, was an eight-hour autonomous shift. By May 17 the run had passed 81 hours and 101,391 packages, [according to the Seoul Economic Daily](https://en.sedaily.com/international/2026/05/17/figure-ai-robot-sorts-100000-packages-in-81-hours-without).

The demon...

Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
